hero.tagline: The original body-on-frame Scorpio, kept tough and simple.

pricing.notes: Mahindra website lists Scorpio Classic range around INR 13.00L - INR 17.00L. Variant-level values should be verified with dealer/state price list before publishing.

overview.minides: Diesel torque, old-school toughness, and a road presence that still makes people move aside. Iykyk.
overview.description: The Mahindra Scorpio Classic is the older-school Scorpio line sold alongside the Scorpio-N. It uses a 2.2L mHawk diesel engine with a 6-speed manual gearbox and 2WD layout. The range is simple: Classic S and Classic S11 trims, with seating choices including 7-side-facing, 9-side-facing and 7-seat captain-chair layouts depending on trim.

suspension.front: Double Wish-bone Type, Independent Front Coil Spring
suspension.rear: Multi Link Coil Spring Suspension with Anti-roll Bar
suspension.shockAbsorbers: Hydraulic Double Acting, Telescopic Shock Absorber

blog.title: Mahindra Scorpio Classic review: old-school SUV appeal with a simple variant line-up
blog.subtitle: Two trims, four seating-led variants, one diesel-manual powertrain.
blog.summary: The Scorpio Classic is for buyers who want the familiar Scorpio shape, rugged road presence and simple diesel-manual mechanicals rather than a tech-heavy crossover experience.

sections.heading: Positioning
body: The Scorpio Classic sits below the Scorpio-N but keeps the traditional Scorpio identity alive. It is aimed at buyers who value road presence, tough construction and high seating capacity.

sections.heading: Engine and performance
body: Every variant uses the 2.2L mHawk diesel engine paired with a 6-speed manual gearbox and 2WD layout. The official output is 97 kW at 3750 r/min and 300 Nm from 1600-2800 r/min.

sections.heading: Variants
body: The range has two trims: Classic S and Classic S11. Classic S focuses on essentials, while Classic S11 adds projector headlamps, DRLs, touchscreen infotainment, automatic temperature control, alloy wheels and more convenience features.

sections.heading: Buyer fit
body: Choose the S if you want the lowest-price Scorpio Classic with seating flexibility. Choose the S11 if you want the proper feature set and more finished exterior/interior equipment.

pros: S11 gets the features most buyers expect
cons: Safety feature list is basic compared with newer SUVs
blog.verdict: The Scorpio Classic is best for buyers who want the classic Scorpio formula: rugged stance, diesel torque and flexible seating. The S11 is the easier recommendation if budget allows.

faqs.question: How many trims does the Scorpio Classic have?
faqs.answer: It has two trims: Classic S and Classic S11.

faqs.question: How many variants are included in this file?
faqs.answer: This JSON includes four variants: S 7SF, S 9SF, S11 7SF and S11 7CC.

faqs.question: Does Scorpio Classic get an automatic gearbox?
faqs.answer: No. The Scorpio Classic is listed with a 6-speed manual gearbox.

metadata.notes: Variant feature data is based on Mahindra Scorpio Classic official page and brochure. Variant-level pricing should be checked against latest city/dealer price list before production use.
